title: Guess The Movie From Just One Meryl Streep Line — No Names, No Mercy
dek: Ten iconic quotes, zero context clues — see how many Meryl Streep classics you can nail before the reveal.

items.quote: But what you don't know is that that sweater is not just blue, it's not turquoise, it's not lapis, it's actually cerulean.
items.reveal_text: The monologue isn't just a burn — the Oscar de la Renta cerulean gown collection Miranda references is a real 2002 moment in fashion history.

items.quote: Don't make me choose! I can't!
items.reveal_text: This is widely considered the single scene that clinched it — Streep won her first Best Actress Oscar for this role.

items.quote: Bottoms up! ... Now, a warning. ... NOW a warning?
items.reveal_text: The delayed-warning gag lands right before the film's groundbreaking effects kick in — work by ILM's Oscar-winning visual effects team that year.
This film pulled off something rare — it became only the fourth movie ever to score four acting nominations without landing a Best Picture nod.

items.quote: Is there anything better than butter?
items.reveal_text: The line channels the real Julia Child's actual food philosophy — she famously told people to use cream if they were scared of butter.

items.quote: I'm his mother. I'm his mother.
items.reveal_text: Streep didn't just perform this courtroom speech — she rewrote it herself, and director Robert Benton said he only cut two of her lines.

items.quote: This kind of certainty comes but just once in a lifetime.
items.reveal_text: The line is lifted almost word for word from Robert James Waller's original bestselling novel that the film was adapted from.

items.quote: I've done a great job with Soph all by myself, and I won't be muscled out by an ejaculation!
items.reveal_text: The film adapted the ABBA jukebox musical that had already been running on the West End since its 1999 debut before Streep ever put on the dungarees.

items.quote: I had a farm in Africa, at the foot of the Ngong Hills.
items.reveal_text: The real farm site became so tied to this story that a developer later named the Nairobi neighborhood built there 'Karen,' after the character.

items.quote: Watch your thoughts, for they become words. Watch your words, for they become actions.
items.reveal_text: Streep's performance as Margaret Thatcher won her the Golden Globe for Best Actress in a Drama.
